In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:New]]

Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of Uncertain Originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Pages to be Translated]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]]

{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|12=draft" to ""
revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

revision.comment: 19 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市) up to rev 100042448
revision.text:
{{簡易区別|倉敷市[[真備町]]服部に在する金毘羅宮（金毘羅大権現）}} {{神社 |名称= 金毘羅大権現 |画像= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 |所在地= [[岡山県]][[倉敷市]][[茶屋町地区|茶屋町]]28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama |祭神= [[金毘羅権現]] |神体= |社格= |創建= 1758年 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''金毘羅大権現'''（こんぴらだいごんげん）は、'''金毘羅宮'''とも称する[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]][[茶屋町地区|茶屋町]]に在する[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == [[1785年]]（宝暦8年）に、当時の庄屋であった平松伴右エ門が、茶屋町地域の安寧と鎮護のために讃岐の象頭山金毘羅大権現（現在の[[金刀比羅宮]]）に願い出て、その御霊を[[分霊|分霊勧請]]する事で成立した神社である。鎮座時より本地の商繁および[[下津井地区|下津井]]より金毘羅本宮参りへ向かう旅人の航路安全を願う神社として深い崇敬を集めた。入口標柱の前に設置された由緒書き看板より入口の標柱門表示や鳥居の扁額は「金毘羅大権現」であるが、拝殿の扁額や行事掲示板には「金毘羅宮」となっている。 同地は[[岡山市|岡山]]を起点として[[早島町|早島]]より'''[[金毘羅街道|金毘羅往来]]'''が横断する地域であり、茶屋町の金毘羅大権現はその沿道に鎮座された。当時のこの地域は[[岡山藩]]の支領であった[[藤戸地区|天城領]]を所領とする[[天城池田家|天城池田氏]]が、本家である[[岡山城]]の池田氏の領地との間を往復する際の中継点として、休憩所を置いた場所であった。そのため金毘羅往来の巡礼者もここで休息をとる事があり、そのための茶屋が多く置かれた。これが「茶屋町」の地名の由来ともされる。茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989年下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981年）平凡社。そのため、この神社はいわゆる「金毘羅参り」の巡礼者の通過点であった。茶屋町の金毘羅大権現は、その巡礼の中盤における中継地点であり金毘羅本宮へ向けた序中盤における巡礼地のひとつとして機能するものである。 周辺地域および巡礼者の崇敬により、[[明治時代|明治期]]の[[廃仏毀釈]]を超えてなお[[真言宗]]系の[[神仏習合]]の名残を残していて、金毘羅大権現と鋳造された釣鐘がかかる鐘楼堂がある。 なお倉敷市域内には[[平成の大合併]]で市域編入となった[[真備町]]などに複数の金毘羅神社が在しており、それぞれに「金毘羅宮」「金毘羅大権現」を社名および通称としているが、これらと茶屋町の金毘羅大権現との間には、同様に琴平の金毘羅宮本社より分霊勧請を願って成立した分社であるという以外の特別なつながりはない。 == 境内 == いわゆる金毘羅神社であるが、それゆえに造りとしては真言宗系[[寺院]]に近いものになっている。境内の北側は地域の小規模な[[遊び場]]のための広場として整備されており[[鉄棒]]や[[ブランコ]]等の[[遊具]]や、[[藤棚]]などの休憩所を見る事ができる。なお広場内には茶屋町地区の治水碑など、街の発展史に関わる碑石がいくつか存在する。 * 標柱門 * 手水場：門外 * 由緒書き等：門外 * [[鳥居]] * 献納灯籠 * 狛犬 * 拝殿・本殿：叶緒には[[鈴]]ではなく[[鰐口]]が据えられている。 * 鐘楼堂 === 摂末社 === * [[稲荷神社|稲荷社]]と庚申天王と大師堂がひとつの建物に連なって鎮座されている。 * [[出雲大社|出雲社]]（祠）：境内の石に小さな鳥居を据え、縁結びの社として鎮座。 * 境内末社（祠）：一畑薬師、淡島大明神、祇園神社、山上大権現、石鎚大権現、和霊大明神、水神宮・・・ == 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] - [[金刀比羅神社]]にとっての総本宮 * [[金毘羅街道]]（[[金毘羅往来]]） * [[瑜伽大権現]]（[[由加神社本宮]]・[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]）- 金毘羅往来の巡礼における次の巡礼所 == 引用・備考 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんひらたいこんけん}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

revision.text:
{{簡易区別|倉敷市[[真備町]]服部に在する金毘羅宮（金毘羅大権現）}} {{神社 |名称= 金毘羅大権現 |画像= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 |所在地= [[岡山県]][[倉敷市]][[茶屋町地区|茶屋町]]28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama |祭神= [[金毘羅権現]] |神体= |社格= |創建= 1758年 |本殿= |別名= |札所等= |例祭= |神事= }} '''金毘羅大権現'''（こんぴらだいごんげん）は、'''金毘羅宮'''とも称する[[岡山県]][[倉敷市]][[茶屋町地区|茶屋町]]に在する[[神社]]である。 == 概要 == [[1785年]]（宝暦8年）に、当時の庄屋であった平松伴右エ門が、茶屋町地域の安寧と鎮護のために讃岐の象頭山金毘羅大権現（現在の[[金刀比羅宮]]）に願い出て、その御霊を[[分霊|分霊勧請]]する事で成立した神社である。鎮座時より本地の商繁および[[下津井地区|下津井]]より金毘羅本宮参りへ向かう旅人の航路安全を願う神社として深い崇敬を集めた。入口標柱の前に設置された由緒書き看板より入口の標柱門表示や鳥居の扁額は「金毘羅大権現」であるが、拝殿の扁額や行事掲示板には「金毘羅宮」となっている。 同地は[[岡山市|岡山]]を起点として[[早島町|早島]]より'''[[金毘羅街道|金毘羅往来]]'''が横断する地域であり、茶屋町の金毘羅大権現はその沿道に鎮座された。当時のこの地域は[[岡山藩]]の支領であった[[藤戸地区|天城領]]を所領とする[[天城池田家|天城池田氏]]が、本家である[[岡山城]]の池田氏の領地との間を往復する際の中継点として、休憩所を置いた場所であった。そのため金毘羅往来の巡礼者もここで休息をとる事があり、そのための茶屋が多く置かれた。これが「茶屋町」の地名の由来ともされる。茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989年下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981年）平凡社。そのため、この神社はいわゆる「金毘羅参り」の巡礼者の通過点であった。茶屋町の金毘羅大権現は、その巡礼の中盤における中継地点であり金毘羅本宮へ向けた序中盤における巡礼地のひとつとして機能するものである。 周辺地域および巡礼者の崇敬により、[[明治時代|明治期]]の[[廃仏毀釈]]を超えてなお[[真言宗]]系の[[神仏習合]]の名残を残していて、金毘羅大権現と鋳造された釣鐘がかかる鐘楼堂がある。 なお倉敷市域内には[[平成の大合併]]で市域編入となった[[真備町]]などに複数の金毘羅神社が在しており、それぞれに「金毘羅宮」「金毘羅大権現」を社名および通称としているが、これらと茶屋町の金毘羅大権現との間には、同様に琴平の金毘羅宮本社より分霊勧請を願って成立した分社であるという以外の特別なつながりはない。 == 境内 == いわゆる金毘羅神社であるが、それゆえに造りとしては真言宗系[[寺院]]に近いものになっている。境内の北側は地域の小規模な[[遊び場]]のための広場として整備されており[[鉄棒]]や[[ブランコ]]等の[[遊具]]や、[[藤棚]]などの休憩所を見る事ができる。なお広場内には茶屋町地区の治水碑など、街の発展史に関わる碑石がいくつか存在する。 * 標柱門 * 手水場：門外 * 由緒書き等：門外 * [[鳥居]] * 献納灯籠 * 狛犬 * 拝殿・本殿：叶緒には[[鈴]]ではなく[[鰐口]]が据えられている。 * 鐘楼堂 === 摂末社 === * [[稲荷神社|稲荷社]]と庚申天王と大師堂がひとつの建物に連なって鎮座されている。 * [[出雲大社|出雲社]]（祠）：境内の石に小さな鳥居を据え、縁結びの社として鎮座。 * 境内末社（祠）：一畑薬師、淡島大明神、祇園神社、山上大権現、石鎚大権現、和霊大明神、水神宮・・・ == 関連項目 == * [[金刀比羅宮]] - [[金刀比羅神社]]にとっての総本宮 * [[金毘羅街道]]（[[金毘羅往来]]） * [[瑜伽大権現]]（[[由加神社本宮]]・[[蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|蓮台寺]]）- 金毘羅往来の巡礼における次の巡礼所 == 引用・備考 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こんひらたいこんけん}} [[Category:倉敷市の神社]] [[Category:金刀比羅神社]]

revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}}
