{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=draft}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|12=draft}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|12=draft}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=draft}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|12=draft}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|12=draft}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=draft}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=draft}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|12=draft}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=draft}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|12=draft}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=draft}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|12=draft}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|12=draft}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|12=draft}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|12=draft}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|12=draft}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|12=draft}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=draft}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|12=draft}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|12=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|12=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|12=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|12=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|12=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|12=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|12=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|12=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|12=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{citation needed|こんひらたいこんけん}} {{citation needed|Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|qq=draft}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|qq=draft}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|qq=draft}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|qq=draft}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|qq=draft}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

revision.comment: Bot: add simplewiki draft link to ill templates
revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1785|qq=}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunrei|qq=}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama City|qq=}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashima Town|qq=}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Domain|qq=}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Castle|qq=}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu-shūgō|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu Kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治時代|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temple|qq=}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/playground|qq=}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/play equipment|qq=}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal bar|qq=}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/swing|qq=}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wanuiguchi|qq=}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wanuiguchi|qq=}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}}（{{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1785|qq=}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunrei|qq=}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama City|qq=}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashima Town|qq=}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Domain|qq=}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Castle|qq=}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu-shūgō|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu Kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temple|qq=}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/playground|qq=}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/play equipment|qq=}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal bar|qq=}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/swing|qq=}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wanuiguchi|qq=}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|ja|金毘羅権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Gongen|qq=}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto|qq=}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1785|qq=}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|ja|分霊|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|lt_ja=分霊勧請|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bunrei|qq=}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama City|ja|岡山市|lt=Okayama|lt_ja=岡山|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama City|qq=}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima Town|ja|早島町|lt=Hayashima|lt_ja=早島|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayashima Town|qq=}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|ja|岡山藩|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Domain|qq=}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|ja|岡山城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Castle|qq=}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|ja|神仏習合|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu-shūgō|qq=}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingon Buddhism|qq=}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu Kishaku|ja|廃仏毀釈|1=Haibutsu kishaku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu Kishaku|13=User:Immanuelle/Haibutsu kishaku|qq=}} of the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|明治|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=明治|lt=Meiji era|lt_ja=明治期|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki City|ja|倉敷市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurashiki City|qq=}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabimachi|ja|真備町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mabimachi|qq=}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|temple|ja|寺院|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/temple|qq=}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|playground|ja|遊び場|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/playground|qq=}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|play equipment|ja|遊具|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/play equipment|qq=}}, such as a {{ill|horizontal bar|ja|鉄棒|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/horizontal bar|qq=}} and a {{ill|swing|ja|ブランコ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/swing|qq=}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|torii|ja|鳥居|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/torii|qq=}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|wanuiguchi|ja|鰐口|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wanuiguchi|qq=}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷神社|lt=Inari|lt_ja=稲荷社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|出雲大社|lt=Izumo Shrine|lt_ja=出雲社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1785|qq=}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:倉敷市の神社]]

(Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 revision.text:
{{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|ja|1785|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1785|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の市町村の廃置分合}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki City]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:Shrines in Kurashiki city]]

{{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の市町村の廃置分合}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]]

However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Konpira Daigongen (Kurashiki City)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Shinto Shrine |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|qq=}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|qq=}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|qq=}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|qq=}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|qq=}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|qq=}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|qq=}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|qq=}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route.
