[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|[[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]][[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]]服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|location= [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|deity= [[:en:Konpira Gongen|Konpira Gongen]] |神体= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = [[:en:Shinto|Shinto]]|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]][[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]]{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== [[:en:1785|1785]]（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the [[:en:Bunrei|Bunrei Enshrinement]] of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]]) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at [[:en:Okayama|Okayama]], is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from [[:en:Hayashima|Hayashima]], and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the [[:en:Okayama Domain|Okayama Domain]], during their travels between their main territories at [[:en:Okayama Castle|Okayama Castle]]. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the [[:en:Shinbutsu-shūgō|Shinbutsu-shūgō]] associated with the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition still persist beyond the [[:en:Haibutsu kishaku|Haibutsu kishaku]] of the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]], as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of [[:en:Kurashiki|Kurashiki City]], several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in [[:en:Mabi, Okayama|Mabimachi]] (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a [[:en:Buddhist temple|temple]] associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local [[:en:Playground|playground]], where one can find various pieces of [[:en:Outdoor playset|play equipment]], such as a [[:en:Horizontal bar|horizontal bar]] and a [[:en:Swing (seat)|swing]], as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * [[:en:Torii|torii]] * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a [[:en:Waniguchi|wanuiguchi]] is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari]], Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * [[:en:Izumo-taisha|Izumo Shrine]] (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * [[:en:Kotohira-gū|Kotohira Shrine]] – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q22118772]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:17, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Gongen}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima|en|Hayashima|WD=Q1155361|lt=Hayashima}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temple}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|Playground|en|Playground|WD=Q11875349|lt=playground}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|Outdoor playset|en|Outdoor playset|WD=Q7111945|lt=play equipment}}, such as a {{ill|Horizontal bar|en|Horizontal bar|WD=Q623270|lt=horizontal bar}} and a {{ill|Swing (seat)|en|Swing (seat)|WD=Q188688|lt=swing}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|WD=Q11674315|lt=wanuiguchi}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

revision.text:
[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Gongen}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima|en|Hayashima|WD=Q1155361|lt=Hayashima}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temple}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|Playground|en|Playground|WD=Q11875349|lt=playground}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|Outdoor playset|en|Outdoor playset|WD=Q7111945|lt=play equipment}}, such as a {{ill|Horizontal bar|en|Horizontal bar|WD=Q623270|lt=horizontal bar}} and a {{ill|Swing (seat)|en|Swing (seat)|WD=Q188688|lt=swing}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|WD=Q11674315|lt=wanuiguchi}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]]

revision.text:
[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Gongen}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima|en|Hayashima|WD=Q1155361|lt=Hayashima}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temple}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|Playground|en|Playground|WD=Q11875349|lt=playground}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|Outdoor playset|en|Outdoor playset|WD=Q7111945|lt=play equipment}}, such as a {{ill|Horizontal bar|en|Horizontal bar|WD=Q623270|lt=horizontal bar}} and a {{ill|Swing (seat)|en|Swing (seat)|WD=Q188688|lt=swing}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|WD=Q11674315|lt=wanuiguchi}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Shrine}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaidō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Rendai-Ji (Kurashiki)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{Simplified Disambiguation|{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}{{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi|1=Mabi, Okayama}}服部に在する{{ill|Konpira Shrine|ja|金毘羅宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金刀比羅宮|1=Kotohira-gū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q94760}}（{{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}}）}} {{Infobox religious building| |名称= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|image= [[File:Chayamachi Konpira-Daigongen.jpg|金毘羅大権現 (茶屋町)|270px]]
茶屋町の金毘羅大権現 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|location= {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}28 |緯度度= 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 25.0 |経度度= 133 |経度分 = 49 | 経度秒 = 07.9 |地図= Japan Okayama | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|deity= {{ill|Konpira Gongen|en|Konpira Gongen|WD=Q167077|lt=Konpira Gongen|1=Konpira Gongen}} |神体= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|type= |創建= 1758th year |本殿= |別名= |札所等= | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|en|Shinto|WD=Q812767|lt=Shinto|1=Shinto}}|festival= |神事= }} '''Konpira Daigongen''' (金毘羅大権現) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} also known as '''Konpira Shrine''', located in {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture|1=Okayama Prefecture}}{{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}{{ill|Chayamachi District|ja|茶屋町地区|lt=Chayamachi|lt_ja=茶屋町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chayamachi District|1=Chayamachi Area|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11617964}}. ==Overview== {{ill|1785|en|1785|WD=Q7756|lt=1785|1=1785}}（宝暦8th year ） saw the establishment of this shrine when the then village head, 平松伴右エ門, prayed at the {{ill|Bunrei|en|Bunrei|WD=Q195793|lt=Bunrei Enshrinement|1=Bunrei}} of the Sanuki象頭山 {{ill|Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Gongen|lt=Konpira Daigongen|ja|金毘羅大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Daigongen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅権現}} (present-day {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}}) for the peace and protection of the Chayamachi area. Since its enshrinement, it has been deeply revered as a shrine that safeguards local commerce and the safe passage of travelers journeying from {{ill|Shimotsui District|ja|下津井地区|lt=Shimotsui|lt_ja=下津井|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimotsui District|1=Shimotsui|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11361095}} to visit the main Konpira shrine.Based on the historical signboard placed in front of the entrance’s stone marker Although the stone marker gate and the inscription on the torii read “Konpira Daigongen,” the inscription on the worship hall and event bulletin board reads “Konpira Shrine.” The area, beginning at {{ill|Okayama|en|Okayama|WD=Q200078|lt=Okayama|1=Okayama}}, is traversed by the '''{{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|lt=Konpira Pilgrimage|lt_ja=金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}}''' from {{ill|Hayashima|en|Hayashima|WD=Q1155361|lt=Hayashima|1=Hayashima}}, and the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi is enshrined along this route. At that time, this region served as a relay point with a resting place for the {{ill|Amagi Ikeda clan|ja|天城池田家|lt=Amagi Ikeda family|lt_ja=天城池田氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagi Ikeda clan|1=Amagi Ikeda clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442460}}, who held the domain of {{ill|Fujido District|ja|藤戸地区|lt=Amagi Domain|lt_ja=天城領|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujido District|1=Fujito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11624064}} under the {{ill|Okayama Domain|en|Okayama Domain|WD=Q903497|lt=Okayama Domain|1=Okayama Domain}}, during their travels between their main territories at {{ill|Okayama Castle|en|Okayama Castle|WD=Q1013448|lt=Okayama Castle|1=Okayama Castle}}. Consequently, many pilgrims on the Konpira pilgrimage would rest here, and numerous tea houses were established for that purpose. This is said to be the origin of the name “Chayamachi.”茶屋町史刊行委員会『改訂版 茶屋町史』1989th year 下中直也 『日本歴史地名体系三四巻　岡山県の地名』（1981st year ）平凡社 Thus, this shrine served as a transit point for pilgrims undertaking the “Konpira pilgrimage.” The Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi functions as an intermediate stop in the early to mid stages of the pilgrimage toward the main Konpira shrine. Owing to the reverence of the surrounding area and its pilgrims, remnants of the {{ill|Shinbutsu-shūgō|en|Shinbutsu-shūgō|WD=Q2279088|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}} associated with the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182|lt=Shingon Buddhism|1=Shingon Buddhism}} tradition still persist beyond the {{ill|Haibutsu kishaku|en|Haibutsu kishaku|WD=Q702298|lt=Haibutsu kishaku|1=Haibutsu kishaku}} of the {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}}, as evidenced by the bell tower hall that houses a cast hanging bell alongside Konpira Daigongen. It is noteworthy that within the area of {{ill|Kurashiki|en|Kurashiki|WD=Q467487|lt=Kurashiki City|1=Kurashiki}}, several Konpira shrines exist—for example, in {{ill|Mabi, Okayama|en|Mabi, Okayama|WD=Q6721577|lt=Mabimachi|1=Mabi, Okayama}} (incorporated during the {{ill|Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja|平成の大合併|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei-era Municipal Mergers|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Japanese cities and townsの廃置分合|1=Municipal mergers and dissolutions in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2411657}})—each using “Konpira Shrine” or “Konpira Daigongen” as their name and common designation. However, apart from being branch shrines established by request for a bunrei from the main Kotohira shrine in Kotohira, there is no special connection with the Konpira Daigongen of Chayamachi. ==Shrine facilities== Although it is a typical Konpira shrine, its structure resembles that of a {{ill|Buddhist temple|en|Buddhist temple|WD=Q5393308|lt=temple|1=Buddhist temple}} associated with the Shingon sect. The northern part of the precincts has been developed into a small local {{ill|Playground|en|Playground|WD=Q11875349|lt=playground|1=Playground}}, where one can find various pieces of {{ill|Outdoor playset|en|Outdoor playset|WD=Q7111945|lt=play equipment|1=Outdoor playset}}, such as a {{ill|Horizontal bar|en|Horizontal bar|WD=Q623270|lt=horizontal bar|1=Horizontal bar}} and a {{ill|Swing (seat)|en|Swing (seat)|WD=Q188688|lt=swing|1=Swing (seat)}}, as well as resting areas like a {{ill|wisteria trellis|ja|藤棚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wisteria trellis|qq=}}. In addition, the playground contains several monuments related to the area’s development history, including the flood-control monument of the Chayamachi District. * Stone Marker Gate * Chōzuya (Purification Fountain): Located outside the gate * Historical signboard and related items: Located outside the gate * {{ill|Torii|en|Torii|WD=Q234731|lt=torii|1=Torii}} * Dedicated Lanterns * Guardian Dogs * Worship Hall/Main Hall: In the main hall, a {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|WD=Q11674315|lt=wanuiguchi|1=Waniguchi}} is installed instead of a {{ill|bell|ja|鈴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bell|1=suzu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q7651136}}. * Bell Tower Hall ===Sessha and Massha=== * A single building complex enshrines the {{ill|Inari shrine|en|Inari shrine|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari|1=Inari shrine}}, Gōshin Tennō, and the Daisidō. * {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|WD=Q696362|lt=Izumo Shrine|1=Izumo-taisha}} (a small shrine): A small torii is set on a stone within the precincts, enshrined as a shrine for matchmaking. * Keidai-Massha (a small shrine): Encompassing deities such as Ichihata Yakushi, Awashima Daimyojin, Gion Shrine, Yamagami Daigongen, Ishizuchi Daigongen, Warai Daimyojin, Suijin-gu, and others. ==See Also== * {{ill|Kotohira-gū|en|Kotohira-gū|WD=Q94760|lt=Kotohira Shrine|1=Kotohira-gū}} – The head shrine for {{ill|Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira shrines|lt=Kotohira Shrine|ja|金刀比羅神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotohira Shrine|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}. * {{ill|Konpira Highway|ja|金毘羅街道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Highway|1=Konpira Kaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}} ({{ill|Konpira Pilgrimage|ja|金毘羅往来|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konpira Pilgrimage|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 金毘羅街道|1=Konpira Kaido|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11647339}}) * {{ill|Yuga Daigongen|ja|瑜伽大権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuga Daigongen|qq=}} ({{ill|Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|ja|由加神社本宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yuka Shrine Main Shrine|1=Yuga Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577571}}・{{ill|Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|ja|蓮台寺 (倉敷市)|lt=Rendaiji|lt_ja=蓮台寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rendaiji (Kurashiki City)|1=Yugasan Rendaiji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11621649}}) – The next stop on the Konpira pilgrimage route. ==References and Notes== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Navigation}} {{Shinto stub}} {{translated page|ja|金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)|version=100042448|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:金毘羅大権現 (倉敷市)]] {{wikidata link|Q22118772}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Kotohira shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:pages]]
